justification:
Harvest & Co deduction HC 20988 claims 30 demo events × $250 = $7,500 under PROMO-2026-Q1-011 (Autumn In-Store Demo Program — Pita & Popcorn). The promotion is confirmed: correct SKUs (MF-SN-001, MF-SN-003), matching rate ($250/event), claim period (10/04–11/15/2025) fully within the promo window, and claimed amount ($7,500) under the $12,000 funding cap. The claim was submitted within the 90-day window (contract §3.3).

However, the claim lacks signed Proof-of-Performance (Exhibit B) forms and formal store execution reports for any of the 30 events. Contract §3.4 requires these documents for each event and states that claims lacking Exhibit B are "not payable as submitted." The remittance note indicates "POP forms to follow," but none were attached.

Execution is partially corroborated by: (1) store photos from 19 stores across Oct/Nov weekends, and (2) POS scan data for PROMO-2026-Q1-011 showing clear scan lift during demo weekends — units scanned during demo weeks (≈2,120–2,220/week) were roughly 75–85% higher than non-demo weeks (≈1,195–1,230/week) for MF-SN-001 and MF-SN-003 combined, consistent with active in-store sampling.

Per Meridian's established demo-billback convention (precedent SH-2025-Q4-007): when Exhibit B documentation is absent but execution is corroborated by both store photos and POS scan lift, the claim settles at 60% of the claimed amount rather than being denied outright. Applying this convention: $7,500 × 60% = $4,500. No duplicate settlement exists for this reference. The $4,500 payment is contingent on Harvest & Co submitting the outstanding Exhibit B forms and execution reports, which Meridian should request promptly.
